label: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 9

extraction.one_line_summary: MENKES Louis won from the breakaway on the brutal final 24% climb while EVENEPOEL Remco distanced ROGLIC Primoz by 1:12 to take the overall lead.
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step controlled peloton tempo for Evenepoel despite losing Serry to COVID, revealing surprising depth in GC support.
Breakaway fractured internally before final climb with Mollema-Janssens work rate disputes, enabling Menkes solo opportunity despite numerical advantage.
Evenepoel attacked on 24% gradients rather than defending, exploiting exact terrain where Roglic's climbing weakness manifests most severely.
Peloton crash before final climb opened gaps that Quick-Step chose not to chase, tactically accepting chaos favored Evenepoel.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: gained 1:12 on Roglic on extreme gradients while appearing comfortable, destroying assumption he only excels in time trials
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: negative: lost over a minute on 3.5km finish climb at 24%, revealing explosive gradient weakness before time trial
rider_signals.MAS Enric: negative: conceded 44 seconds to Evenepoel despite being pure climber, signals insufficient form to challenge in mountains
rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: finished 2:37 down despite winning this stage in 2018, massive decline on explosive finishes
rider_signals.VINE Jay: neutral: fifth place after two prior stage wins suggests breakaway specialist struggling when GC riders engage
rider_signals.DEGE Thymen Arensman: negative: DSM refused to let him in break despite being 3:18 down, bizarre tactical conservatism that wasted opportunity
